Motion To Lift Stay Pending Arbitration And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-17-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JAHAN P. RAISSI of Shartsis Friese LLP, Tel No. (415) 421-6500 appearing for Defendants Medcap Management & Research LLC, Medcap Partners, L.P., and Charles Frederick Toney Jr.;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS MARK SOLE AND ADINA SAFER'S Motion To Lift Stay Pending Arbitration And Request For Sanctions is denied. "[A] trial court may not lift a stay of litigation merely because a party cannot afford the costs associated with arbitration." (MKJA, Inc. v. 123 Fit Franchising, LLC (2011) 191 Cal.App.4th 643, 660.) Plaintiffs' arguments regarding delay and insufficient discovery responses must be raised to the arbitrator. "The arbitrator, and not the court, decides questions of procedure and discovery." (Titan Value Equities Group, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1994) 29 Cal.App.4th 482, 488.) Further, "it is also up to the arbitrator, not the court, to grant relief for delay in bringing the arbitration to a resolution." (Id.) JAMS rules provide that a plaintiff may advance the arbitration fees so that the arbitration may proceed. If the plaintiff prevails, the fees are added to the award. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/EHG)
